rationale: The cited paper is thematically relevant because it provides a customer-based firm valuation framework and argues customers are intangible assets, while the citing paper studies how customer satisfaction information translates into firm value outcomes in a marketing-finance context. This makes a substantive linkage plausible (foundational/background theory on customer metrics and valuation), but the provided evidence includes only a single DOI-matched reference with no citation-context text showing direct methodological or theoretical use. Given this mixed strength, the pair is not safe to drop but not clearly keep.
